summary: Blazor WASM apps targeting .NET 10 fail at runtime with TypeInitializationException (SKImageInfo) or DllNotFoundException (libSkiaSharp) because SkiaSharp's pre-compiled emscripten static libraries (built for 3.1.56) may be incompatible with .NET 10's updated emscripten toolchain, and the wasm-tools workload or WasmBuildNative configuration may not be correctly set up.

stepsToReproduce: Create a Blazor WebAssembly app targeting net10.0
Add an SKCanvasView component with a simple OnPaintSurface handler
Run the app and navigate to the page with the component
Observe TypeInitializationException or DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Windows 11 Pro, VS 2026, .NET 10, Edge/Chrome; also reported on macOS with Rider and Linux in production

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SkiaSharp has not yet shipped a .NET 10-aware release. PR #3514 (open) adds .NET 10 SDK support. The WASM static library condition for net9.0+ should technically match net10.0, but emscripten toolchain version mismatch is the likely root cause.

regression.reason: Works on .NET 8 and .NET 9 but fails on .NET 10. Multiple users confirm rolling back to .NET 8 resolves the issue. The regression is in the .NET SDK/emscripten toolchain interaction, not in SkiaSharp code itself.

analysis.summary: The .NET 10 WASM build toolchain likely ships with a newer version of emscripten than 3.1.56, which is the version SkiaSharp's static libraries are compiled against for net9.0+. WASM uses static linking (not dynamic P/Invoke), so the .a files must be ABI-compatible with the emscripten toolchain used by the .NET SDK. Additionally, SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor only declares Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web conditions up to net9.0, and global.json pins SDK to 8.0.100, meaning official .NET 10 support has not yet been added.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly a bug — multiple independent reporters get the same DllNotFoundException/TypeInitializationException on .NET 10 with a simple repro. The issue is in the build/packaging layer, not user code. Community workarounds (wasm-tools workload, explicit NativeAssets.WebAssembly reference, WasmBuildNative/RunAOTCompilation flags) are inconsistent, suggesting the root cause is emscripten ABI mismatch that varies by environment configuration.

keySignals.interpretation: SKImageInfo static constructor failed — native library could not be loaded at all.
Confirms the native static library was not linked into dotnet.wasm — the emscripten build step failed or was skipped.

keySignals.text: Rolling back to .NET8 and it works again however. Same version of SkiaSharp 3.119.2
keySignals.interpretation: Same SkiaSharp version works on .NET 8 but not .NET 10, confirming the issue is in the SDK/toolchain interaction.

keySignals.text: Removing WasmBuildNative false ... And in a different project adding RunAOTCompilation false
keySignals.interpretation: WasmBuildNative must be true for native linking. If explicitly set to false, the static libraries are never linked.
The wasm-tools workload provides the emscripten toolchain needed for native linking. Missing workload prevents the build from linking .a files.

codeInvestigation.finding: The WasmBuildNative property is set to true by default (line 27), and NativeFileReference for net9.0+ uses VersionGreaterThanOrEquals(9.0) which should match net10.0. However, the static libraries are compiled with emscripten 3.1.56, which may not be ABI-compatible with .NET 10's emscripten toolchain.

The _SkiaSharpRuntimeIssue109289Workaround target reorders libSkiaSharp.a during linking — a workaround for dotnet/runtime#109289. If .NET 10 changes the linking pipeline, this workaround may no longer function correctly.
Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web version conditions only go up to net9.0. No net10.0 condition exists, though TFM fallback should handle this. The project uses $(TFMNext) which is currently empty.
TFMCurrent is net8.0, TFMNext is empty — SkiaSharp has not yet added .NET 10 as a target. PR #3514 is the pending update.

workarounds: Run 'dotnet workload restore' to ensure the wasm-tools workload is installed (worked for some users)
Add explicit PackageReference for SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly alongside SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor
Remove WasmBuildNative=false if present, and add RunAOTCompilation=false to the project
Pin to .NET 8 or .NET 9 SDK via global.json until SkiaSharp ships .NET 10 support
nextQuestions: Which emscripten version does .NET 10 ship with, and is it ABI-compatible with the 3.1.56 static libraries?
Does PR #3514 (.NET 10 SDK update) resolve the WASM linking issue?
Are there additional emscripten version-specific static libraries needed for .NET 10?

resolution.hypothesis: .NET 10's emscripten toolchain version is incompatible with the pre-compiled static libraries (emscripten 3.1.56) shipped in SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly. The wasm-tools workload may also have changed in .NET 10, requiring updated build integration.

proposals.title: Install wasm-tools workload and ensure WasmBuildNative is not disabled
proposals.description: Run 'dotnet workload restore' and verify the project does not set WasmBuildNative=false. Add RunAOTCompilation=false if WasmBuildNative was previously false.
proposals.codeSnippet:
<!-- In .csproj -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <RunAOTCompilation>false</RunAOTCompilation>
  <!-- Do NOT set WasmBuildNative to false -->
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly" Version="3.119.2" />
  <PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor" Version="3.119.2" />
</ItemGroup>

proposals.title: Pin SDK to .NET 9 via global.json
proposals.description: Add a global.json pinning the SDK to .NET 9.x until SkiaSharp officially supports .NET 10. This is the most reliable workaround as the issue is specific to .NET 10's emscripten toolchain.

proposals.title: Add .NET 10 emscripten-compatible static libraries
proposals.description: Build and ship libSkiaSharp.a compiled with the emscripten version used by .NET 10's wasm-tools workload, and add a new NativeFileReference condition for TargetFrameworkVersion 10.0 in the .targets file. This is the proper fix, tracked in PR #3514.

resolution.recommendedReason: Most reliable workaround with highest confidence. The wasm-tools workaround only works for some users, suggesting the root cause varies by environment. Pinning to .NET 9 avoids the incompatibility entirely until PR #3514 ships official .NET 10 support.

actionability.reason: Real bug affecting multiple users. PR #3514 is open to add .NET 10 support but not yet merged. Need to confirm emscripten version compatibility and potentially ship new static libraries.

actions.description: Fix platform label (os/Windows-Universal-UWP is incorrect, this is a WASM issue)
Acknowledge the issue and provide workaround guidance
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed report. This is a known compatibility gap — SkiaSharp hasn't shipped official .NET 10 support yet. The WASM static libraries (`.a` files) are compiled with emscripten 3.1.56 for .NET 9, and .NET 10 may ship with an incompatible emscripten version.

Here's the most reliable workaround while we work on .NET 10 support: pin your project to .NET 9 via `global.json`:

```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "9.0.200",
    "rollForward": "latestMinor"
  }
}
```

If you need to stay on .NET 10, try these steps (results vary by environment):
1. Run `dotnet workload restore` to ensure wasm-tools is installed
2. Add an explicit `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly` PackageReference
3. Make sure `WasmBuildNative` is **not** set to `false` in your `.csproj`
4. If you had `WasmBuildNative=false`, remove it and add `<RunAOTCompilation>false</RunAOTCompilation>` instead

.NET 10 support is being tracked in PR #3514.
